GenoSim 1.0.0

  • Articles
    • Introduction to GenoSim: Forward-Time Genotype Simulation

Hardy-Weinberg Equilibrium Test

Source: R/analysis.R
hwe_test.Rd

Performs a chi-squared goodness-of-fit test for Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium at each SNP using observed genotype counts versus expected counts under HWE. Loci that are fixed (monomorphic) are flagged as NA rather than tested.

hwe_test(geno_matrix, alpha = 0.05)

Arguments

geno_matrix

Integer matrix of dosage values (0/1/2), dimensions \(n_{individuals} \times n_{SNPs}\). Rownames = sample IDs, colnames = SNP IDs.

alpha

Numeric. Significance level for the HWE test. Default 0.05.

Value

A data.frame with one row per SNP and columns:

snp_id

SNP identifier.

chi2

Chi-squared test statistic (1 degree of freedom).

p_value

P-value from \(\chi^2(1)\) distribution.

hwe_pass

Logical. TRUE if p_value >= alpha.

sig_label

Character: "HWE_pass", "HWE_deviation", or "fixed".

See also

simulate_population, compute_ld

Examples

sim <- simulate_population(n_founders = 100, n_snps = 200,
                           n_generations = 1, seed = 1)
#> === GenoSim: Population Forward Simulator ===
#>   Founders: 100 | SNPs: 200 | Generations: 1
#>   F=0.000 | mu=1.00e-04 | s=0.000 | Ne=100
#> [1/4] Simulating founder genotypes...
#> [2/4] Running forward simulation...
#>   -> Generation 1/1
#> [3/4] Computing summary statistics...
#> 
#> === Simulation Complete ===
#>   Total individuals: 250 | SNPs: 200
hwe <- hwe_test(sim$genotypes[[1]])
table(hwe$sig_label)
#> 
#> HWE_deviation      HWE_pass 
#>            13           187
